core_mechanism: A quantum-to-classical (Rokhsar-Kivelson-type) correspondence in which a system is tuned to an anisotropic-scaling critical point (a Lifshitz point with dynamical critical exponent z=2, where one distinguished coordinate scales quadratically relative to the others) so that the squared amplitude of its ground state reproduces exactly the partition function of a statistical/field-theory model living in one fewer dimension.
brief_reason: The abstract's central design goal is that the ground-state wavefunction of the higher-dimensional theory 'reproduces the partition function' of the lower-dimensional system, achieved by placing both the matter and gravity sectors at a z=2 Lifshitz critical point.
